title: Pristimera luteoviridis
taxonomy: Pristimera luteoviridis is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and is also known by its synonym, Euonymus luteoviridis. The plant belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is further classified as a dicotyledonous angiosperm. Pristimera luteoviridis is a woody climber that has been used in traditional medicine in some parts of its native region. Its classification places it in the family Celastraceae, which is commonly known as the staff vine family.
uses: Pristimera luteoviridis, a plant species native to tropical Africa, has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. The plant's extracts have been reported to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal uses.
